frameworks.name: deep neural network / connectionist
frameworks.one_line: Standard SGD-trained network augmented with biologically motivated constraints.

frameworks.name: prefrontal context gating
frameworks.one_line: Hebbian task-to-hidden gating produces orthogonal task representations.

frameworks.name: continual learning
frameworks.one_line: Catastrophic-interference framing motivating blocked vs interleaved curricula.

core_question: What computational constraints let a neural network learn sequential tasks without catastrophic interference, matching human curriculum effects?
key_assumptions: Humans suffer less interference under blocked than interleaved training
Standard SGD networks show the opposite pattern
Prefrontal-style task gating can be modeled at the unit level
manipulations: Two algorithmic motifs added to SGD: sluggish (exponentially decaying) task units and a Hebbian step
key_findings: Sluggish task units introduce a switch-cost that biases interleaved training toward a joint, context-ignoring representation
The Hebbian step builds a task-to-hidden gating scheme yielding orthogonal, interference-free representations
The augmented network matches human behavioural data under blocked and interleaved curricula
interpretations: Exponentially decaying task signals plus Hebbian context gating jointly capture the human cost of interleaved training
Two simple biologically inspired motifs are sufficient to reproduce curriculum-dependent continual learning in humans
